races.name: Ultra-X Scotland
races.elevation_notes: Difficulty 2/6, Technicality 2/5. Runs along Loch Ness and Scottish Highlands.
races.notes: UTMB World Series indexed. Charity entries available at ~50% discount. Under-23s enter free.

races.name: Great Divide Ultra
races.elevation_notes: Significant climbing at altitude in Mueller State Park
races.notes: Mueller State Park. Multiple start options for 50K. Night race variants available. Historically sells out.

races.name: Cody Peak 50K (Teton Mountain Runs)
races.elevation_notes: ~9,700 ft total gain for 50K. Summits Cody Peak and Rendezvous Mountain.
races.notes: Stunning views of the Grand Tetons. Multiple distances across the weekend.

races.name: Cayuga Trails 50
races.elevation_notes: Very technical East Coast trail with gorges, waterfalls, and historic stone stairs. ~95% trail.
races.notes: Robert H. Treman & Buttermilk Falls State Parks. Often considered one of the most scenic mid-Atlantic ultras. Capped at ~400 runners.

races.name: McKenzie River Trail Run
races.elevation_notes: ~2,200-2,500 ft gain. Very runnable point-to-point trail.
races.notes: Oregon's oldest ultramarathon (since 1988). Point-to-point from Carmen Reservoir to McKenzie Community Track. Shuttle required to start. Cupless event. 8-hour cutoff. $115 via UltraSignup.

races.name: Kettle Moraine
races.elevation_notes: Rolling terrain, moderate climbing on Ice Age Trail.
races.notes: 65 miles on the Ice Age Trail. Lottery drawn January 1 at 11am Central. Running since 1996.

races.name: Speedgoat 50K
races.elevation_notes: ~11,000+ ft gain. Very steep, technical mountain terrain at Snowbird ski resort.
races.notes: One of the most challenging 50Ks in the US. High altitude, steep technical terrain. Fills fast.

races.name: Grand Targhee Wildflower 50K
races.elevation_notes: High-elevation mountain course with significant climbing at Grand Targhee Resort.
races.notes: Part of Targhee Fest weekend. Beautiful wildflower-covered mountain terrain.

races.name: Wy'east Wonder
races.elevation_notes: 50K: 4,300' gain / 7,200' loss. 50M: 7,600' gain / 10,600' loss. Point-to-point on Mt Hood ridges.
races.notes: Point-to-point on the east side of Mt Hood. Start at Bennett Pass Sno Park. Early bird $155 (Dec 1-14), then $175.

races.name: Catamount Ultra
races.elevation_notes: ~5,000 ft total gain/loss for 50K (two loops of 25K course). 9-hour cutoff.
races.notes: Trapp Family Lodge, Stowe VT. Organized by Ironwood Adventure Works.

races.name: Pico Duarte Ultra
races.elevation_notes: Massive elevation gain. 100K: ~23,600 ft. 62K: ~15,500 ft. Cordillera Central mountains.
races.notes: Summits Pico Duarte, the highest peak in the Caribbean. Jarabacoa region. 46K is a run up the summit and down.

races.name: Ghost Train Rail Trail Race
races.elevation_notes: ~300 ft per 15-mile loop. Very flat rail trail.
races.notes: Spooky race! Unique format: choose any increment of 15 miles. Extremely popular, sells out almost instantly on registration day.

races.name: Charlotte Marathon
races.elevation_notes: ~1,040 ft total gain. Rolling, consistently hilly course.
races.notes: Saturday race (not Sunday). Good BQ course despite hills.

races.name: Twin Cities Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Mostly flat to gently rolling. Notable hills around miles 20-23 (~550 ft total).
races.notes: Point-to-point from Minneapolis to Saint Paul. Known as 'The Most Beautiful Urban Marathon in America.'

races.name: Detroit Free Press Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Mostly flat. ~388 ft gain with bridge and tunnel climbs.
races.notes: Unique course crosses into Windsor, Canada via the Ambassador Bridge and returns through the Detroit-Windsor Tunnel.

races.name: Philadelphia Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Flat and fast.
races.notes: Fast, flat course through historic Philadelphia. Good BQ course.

races.name: Berlin Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Extremely flat. One of the flattest major marathons in the world.
races.notes: World Marathon Major. Fastest marathon course in the world (multiple world records set here). Lottery + charity/tour operator options.

races.name: Chicago Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Very flat. ~180 ft gain.
races.notes: World Marathon Major. Fast, flat course through 29 neighborhoods. Lottery window closes Nov 18.

races.name: Marine Corps Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Rolling, moderately hilly compared to flat races. ~700 ft total gain.
races.notes: Known as 'The People's Marathon.' No prize money. Passes major D.C. monuments.

races.name: Indianapolis Monumental Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Flat and fast. ~300 ft total gain.
races.notes: Saturday race. Known as a great BQ course due to flat, fast profile.

races.name: Wineglass Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Net downhill (~200 ft drop). Very fast course.
races.notes: Point-to-point net downhill course through Finger Lakes wine country. One of the fastest BQ courses in the Northeast.

races.name: New York City Marathon
races.elevation_notes: ~810 ft total gain. Rolling, bridge-heavy course.
races.notes: World Marathon Major. All five boroughs. Lottery window Feb 4-25, drawing March 4. 9+1 guaranteed entry program available through NYRR.

races.name: London Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Largely flat course. ~330 ft total gain.
races.notes: World Marathon Major. TCS sponsored. Ballot window opens late April for following year's edition. Starts Greenwich/Blackheath, finishes on The Mall near Buckingham Palace. ~50,000 runners. Charity places available.

races.name: Tokyo Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Very flat. ~130 ft total gain. One of the fastest courses in the world.
races.notes: World Marathon Major. 40,000 runners. General lottery: Aug 14-28, results announced mid-September. Starts at Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (Shinjuku), finishes near Tokyo Station.

races.name: Sydney Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Moderately hilly. ~650-820 ft total gain. Crosses Sydney Harbour Bridge.
races.notes: Abbott World Marathon Majors member. Crosses Harbour Bridge, passes Opera House, finishes in Royal Botanic Gardens. Southern Hemisphere spring race.

races.name: Cherry Blossom 10 Mile / 5K
races.elevation_notes: ~270-300 ft gain. Flat, fast course along the Tidal Basin.
races.notes: Beautiful course during peak cherry blossom season. 10 mile is lottery; 5K may have direct entry depending on year.

races.name: BAA 5K
races.elevation_notes: Generally flat to rolling.
races.notes: Part of Boston Marathon weekend. Run by the Boston Athletic Association. Fills quickly.

races.name: BAA 10K
races.elevation_notes: Generally flat to rolling.
races.notes: Run by the Boston Athletic Association. Summer race. Fills quickly.

races.name: BAA Half Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Rolling. Franklin Park area.
races.notes: Presented by Dana-Farber/Jimmy Fund. Fills VERY quickly (sold out in ~2 hours in past years). Set calendar reminders well in advance.

races.name: Cambridge Half Marathon
races.elevation_notes: ~200-300 ft gain. Flat, fast course along Charles River.
races.notes: Starts/finishes at CambridgeSide. Course along Memorial Drive with views of MIT, Harvard. Over $1.7M donated to charity since 2017.

races.name: Route 66 Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Rolling hills.
races.notes: Sunday before Thanksgiving weekend in Tulsa.

races.name: Falmouth Road Race
races.elevation_notes: ~300 ft gain. Short but warm and rolling seaside course.
races.notes: Cape Cod classic. Falmouth residents: May 1-7. General lottery: May 8-17. Charity entries also available.

races.name: Mount Washington Road Race
races.elevation_notes: ~4,650 ft gain over 7.6 miles. Extremely steep. Average grade ~12%.
races.notes: One of the most iconic hill climb races in the US. Summits Mt Washington (6,288 ft).

races.name: Harpoon 5-Miler
races.elevation_notes: ~30-40 ft gain. Very flat, fast course.
races.notes: 21+ event. Post-race beer at Harpoon Brewery. Benefits ALS research (Angel Fund + Pete Frates Foundation). Saturday race.

races.name: Western States Endurance Run
races.elevation_notes: 18,090 ft gain / 22,970 ft descent. Squaw Valley to Auburn on the Western States Trail.
races.notes: The oldest 100-mile trail race. Lottery entry requires qualification via approved races. Odds improve with consecutive unsuccessful entries. Lottery drawn first Saturday in December.

races.name: Leadville Trail 100
races.elevation_notes: ~15,600 ft gain. Elevation ranges 9,200-12,620 ft. Crosses Hope Pass twice.
races.notes: Out-and-back course over Hope Pass (12,620 ft). No formal qualifier required but 30-hour cutoff is strict. Lottery typically opens in fall/early winter.

races.name: Leadville Trail Marathon & Heavy Half
races.elevation_notes: Summits Mosquito Pass at 13,185 ft. Highest trail marathon in the USA.
races.notes: LT100 Run qualifier event. Summits Mosquito Pass (13,185 ft), highest continuous pass in the country. Marathon cutoff 9.5 hrs, Heavy Half cutoff 7 hrs. First-come first-served registration opens Jan 9 at 10am MST.

races.name: Silver Rush 50 Run
races.elevation_notes: 7,500 ft gain over 50 miles through historic mining district.
races.notes: LT100 Run qualifier event. Out-and-back course. 14-hour cutoff (6am-8pm). First-come first-served registration opens Jan 9 at 10am MST.

races.name: Leadville 10K Run
races.elevation_notes: 475 ft gain. Uses first and last 3.1 miles of LT100 Run course.
races.notes: Shortest race in the Leadville Race Series. Held during LT100 race week. Not a qualifier event.

races.name: Grandma's Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Point-to-point, net downhill from Two Harbors to Duluth along the North Shore of Lake Superior.
races.notes: Point-to-point along scenic North Shore of Lake Superior. Fast BQ course. ~9,000 marathon runners.

races.name: Hamptons Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Flat, fast course on the East End of Long Island.
races.notes: Late September on Long Island's East End. Flat and scenic.

races.name: Broad Street Run
races.elevation_notes: Nearly flat, slightly net downhill point-to-point along Broad Street.
races.notes: Largest 10-mile road race in the US (~40,000 runners). First Sunday in May. Point-to-point from Central High School to the Navy Yard. Lottery registration.

races.name: Eugene Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Mostly flat along Willamette River bike paths with some rolling sections.
races.notes: BQ qualifying, USATF certified. TrackTown USA. Starts at Autzen Stadium, runs along Willamette River.

races.name: Oklahoma City Memorial Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Generally flat single-loop course through downtown OKC with modest rolling terrain.
races.notes: BQ qualifying, USATF sanctioned. Commemorates the 1995 OKC bombing. Starts at the National Memorial & Museum. 24,000+ runners.

races.name: Bandit Grand Prix
races.elevation_notes: Flat. Venue event.
races.notes: Formula One-inspired running event held at the Brooklyn Storehouse in New York's Navy Yard. Indoor/outdoor course lined with lights, music, and spectators, and capped by a podium celebration.

races.name: New Balance Bronx 10 Mile
races.elevation_notes: Through the Bronx with moderate elevation changes.
races.notes: Part of NYRR Five-Borough Series. Qualifies for NYC Marathon (9+1 program) and NYC Half (4 of 6 program). Sells out.

races.name: Tulsa Run
races.elevation_notes: Generally flat to rolling course through Tulsa.
races.notes: Tulsa tradition since 1978. 150,000+ 15K finishers over its history. Same organizers as Route 66 Marathon. Held in late October.

races.name: Jersey City Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Mostly Flat
races.notes: BQ qualifying, USATF certified. Extremely flat course. 10,000+ runners from 50+ countries.

races.name: Bayshore Marathon
races.elevation_notes: Flat and fast out-and-back along Grand Traverse Bay. 100% paved, shaded.
races.notes: Memorial Day Weekend Saturday. Founded 1983. Great BQ course. Sells out.

races.name: 7 Sisters Trail Race
races.elevation_notes: 3,500+ ft gain in 12 miles. Out-and-back on the Holyoke Range ridgecrest.
races.notes: Widely considered the most challenging trail race in the Northeast. Steep, technical singletrack on the M-M Trail. Sells out fast. Runners must carry own hydration.
